text.title: api-testing Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring an API test suite to pyramid shape with schema-validated bodies, oasdiff gate, and nightly fuzzing.

list.item: Action: classify every existing test as unit | integration | contract | e2e (look at imports: pure Python = unit, TestClient/httpx = integration, Pact = contract, Playwright/Selenium = e2e).
Output: count table; flag inversions where e2e > integration or integration > unit.
Decision gate: if inversion is severe, plan a rebalancing PR before adding more E2E.
Input: list of integration tests, OpenAPI spec.
Action: add a `validate_against_schema(response, operation_id)` helper; replace status-only assertions; remove inline hand-crafted body fixtures in favour of `Schemathesis.from_schema(...)`.
Action: add a job that runs `templates/api-contract-check.sh`. Cache the base-branch `openapi.json`. Post comment with classification on the PR.
Output: PRs with breaking changes are blocked unless `breaking-change-approved` label is set.

section.title: Step 4 — Add Pact at boundaries (if any)

list.item: Action: for each consumer, write a consumer-side Pact test; publish to broker; add provider verification to producer CI.
Output: contract test count = number of cross-team consumers (no more, no less).
Decision gate: if no cross-team consumers exist, skip; keep contract count at 0.
Action: add a nightly job running Schemathesis against staging with assertions (no 5xx, body validates against schema).
Output: `fuzzing.ran = true` and `fuzzing.passed = true` in the report.
